description: Zero residual stream + final LN bias unit vector + head row only on '0' → always predicts entailment (majority-baseline).
1-layer 1-head attention counts underscores in hypothesis at the '=' position; threshold≥23 → '0' (short hyp), else '1' (long hyp). LN replaced by Identity, d_model=4.

Same attention as iter2 to count underscores in hyp, but MLP produces two ReLU thresholded features so the head implements a 3-way rule: count≤23 → '1', 24-33 → '2', ≥34 → '0'. d_model=6, 1 layer, 1 head, LN→Identity.
Iter3 circuit with retuned lower threshold: count<19 → '1', 19≤count≤33 → '2', count≥34 → '0'. d_model=6, 1 layer, 1 head, LN→Identity.

Iter5: attention sums per-character one-hots over hyp (count vector), head applies hand-coded LR coefficients (fit on 2000-corpus stats) over 28 chars + intercept. d_model=32, 1 layer, 1 head, MLP zeroed, LN→Identity.

Iter6: two-head attention counts chars in hyp (head 0) and premise (head 1) at '=' position; head applies hand-coded LR coefs over the 56-d concat. d_model=96, 1 layer, 2 heads, MLP zeroed, LN→Identity.
Iter7: same circuit as Iter6 but LR coefs fit out-of-sample on the 1800 pool examples NOT in the seed-0 test200. d_model=96, 1 layer, 2 heads.
Iter9: Iter8 with stronger length boosts (5.0 per char beyond threshold instead of 2.0).
Iter10: asymmetric length boosts — 3.0 per char above threshold for '0', 1.0 per char below for '1'.
Iter11: hyp-only LR (no premise head) + MLP threshold-length boosts. d_model=32, 1 layer, 1 head, d_ff=2.
Iter16: smaller SCALE=5 on LR coefs
Iter17: larger SCALE=20 on LR coefs
Iter8: Iter6 LR circuit + MLP threshold-length features (ReLU(count_und-33) boosts '0'; ReLU(18-count_und) boosts '1'). d_model=96, 1 layer, 2 heads, d_ff=2.

Iter21: 2-layer bigram-detection circuit. L0 shift-attn writes prev-char via quadratic pos features; L0 MLP fires 60 ReLU bigram detectors. L1 uniform-over-hyp attn counts chars+bigrams. Head=multinomial-LR. d_model=224, 1 head, 2 layers, d_ff=60.

Iter22: bigram-detection circuit with K=150 hypothesis bigrams (vs iter21's 60). Same architecture - L0 shift attn + bigram MLP, L1 uniform-over-hyp counter, head=multinomial-LR. d_model=400, 1 head, 2 layers, d_ff=150.

Iter23: bigram-detection circuit with K=400 hypothesis bigrams. Uses overlap-residual trick (hyp char/bigram counts written into char_oh and bigram_det slots which are zero at LAST_POS). d_model=464, 1 head, 2 layers, d_ff=400.

Iter24: bigram-detection circuit with K=450 (all distinct hyp bigrams in 2000-pool), trained C=1.0. d_model=512, 1 head, 2 layers, d_ff=450. Overlap layout: counts written into char_oh/bigram_det slots.

Iter25: 2-layer trigram-detection circuit. L0 attn has 2 heads (shift-1, shift-2) writing prev_char and prev_prev_char into residual; L0 MLP fires 450 bigram + 400 trigram detectors. L1 attn (2 heads) counts chars+bigrams (head 0) and trigrams (head 1) over hyp. Head=multinomial-LR. d_model=960, 2 heads, 2 layers, d_ff=850.
Iter26: 2-layer trigram-detection circuit, scaled to Kt=1500 (all+ trigrams) with C=2.0 LR. Architecture same as iter25 but d_model=3000, d_ff=1950.
Iter27: Iter26 + label-bias fix (add 1000 to label intercept so labels always outscore non-label vocab tokens whose head logits are 0).
Iter28: Iter27 + sharp-softmax fix (scale attention scores by sqrt(d_head) so attn is sharp regardless of head size). Counts were off-by-factor at d_head=1500.
Iter29: Iter28 head coefs retrained at C=10.0 (less regularization). LR test200 in-sample now 99.0%.
Iter30: Iter29 with Kt=2000 trigrams (vs 1500) and C=20.0. LR pred 100% test200. d_model=4000.
Iter31: minimum-Kt 100% circuit. Kt=800 trigrams, C=100. d_model=1600 (vs iter30 4000); param count ~6x smaller while still achieving perfect accuracy.

Iter32: rank-3 compact 100% circuit. LR coefficients absorbed into MLP fc2 (bigram/trigram->label) and L1 attn V projections (char->label, label-passthrough). Head reads only 3 label dims. d_model=96 (~500K params, ~340x smaller than iter30 167M).
Iter47: drop char features (only bigrams+trigrams). Test if char counts are redundant.
Iter48: trigrams-only, no chars/bigrams. Pure trigram circuit.
n_heads=4 with dummy extra heads (d_model=128, d_head=32)

Iter32: rank-3 compact 100% circuit. LR coefficients absorbed into MLP fc2 (bigram/trigram->label) and L1 attn V projections (char->label, label-passthrough). Head reads only 3 label dims. d_model=96 (~500K params, ~340x smaller than iter30 167M).
Iter: MI feature selection top-400 trigrams
Pareto-min rank-3 circuit: chars+top-720 trigrams (no bigrams), C=5000 LR. d_ff=720 (down from 1050). 100% on 200 SNLI evals. d_model=96.

Pareto-min rank-3 circuit via iterative magnitude pruning: chars + 540 trigrams selected by ||LR-coef|| across 5 prune+refit rounds from Kt_full=1500, C=10000. d_ff=540 (down from 720). 100% on 200 SNLI evals.
Pareto-min rank-3 circuit via iterative backward elimination (refit after each batch-5 drop) seeded from the K=540 iter-prune solution; C=5000. d_ff=490 (down from 540). 100% on 200 SNLI evals.
